reasoning: The question depends on whether a vaccine for Vibrio vulnificus infection will have been developed according to the English Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases" by three resolution dates in early 2026. The most recent known value as of late December 2025 was "No." Development and regulatory approval of a human vaccine require multi-year clinical trials. Current research indicates that while some experimental vaccines exist for aquaculture (fish) and early-stage studies have occurred in mice, there is no late-stage clinical trial for a human vaccine for V. vulnificus nearing completion. Consequently, the actual development and deployment of a recognized vaccine before April 2026 is highly improbable. The primary path to a 'Yes' resolution would be an inaccurate or vandalism edit to the Wikipedia article that happens to be live precisely on the resolution dates. Given the stringent moderation of biomedical articles on Wikipedia and the obscurity of this specific row, the chance of an uncorrected erroneous "Yes" is very low. Probabilities are set to the minimum threshold, widening slightly for later dates to account for the growing cumulative window of potential anomalous Wikipedia edits.
